title: The Bananawani Threat
description: On pages 5–8, this part focuses on the bananawani threat, keeping the scene centered on the conflict, choices, and stakes of the Alabasta arc.
scene: The Bananawani Threat. Sanji’s hidden role as Mr. Prince disrupts Crocodile’s plan and creates the opening the crew needs to escape. This section presents the immediate scene beat before the dialogue continues.

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: The Royal Guards hurt a little kid!?

dialogue.speaker: Crocodile
dialogue.text: Don't worry, we'll stop the bleeding soon!

dialogue.speaker: Crocodile
dialogue.text: Don't try to talk or you might make it worse!

dialogue.speaker: Crocodile
dialogue.text: Damn those bastards! How could they hurt a little kid!?

dialogue.speaker: Crocodile
dialogue.text: Those weren't the real royal guards!

dialogue.speaker: Crocodile
dialogue.text: We need a doctor over here!

dialogue.speaker: Chopper
dialogue.text: To put an end to this country...!

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: And tell them to prepare for the final battle...

dialogue.speaker: Miss All Sunday
dialogue.text: But Kouza! We still don't have enough weapons!

dialogue.speaker: Smoker
dialogue.text: Not quite! The huge ship that just crashed into the port was a trade ship stocked full of weapons and monitors!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: It's like a sign from the heavens...

dialogue.speaker: Baroque Works agent
dialogue.text: As long as Mr. 4 and his partner have done their job...

dialogue.speaker: Miss All Sunday
dialogue.text: Don't worry, that fatass and cranky granny can pull through when it matters!

dialogue.speaker: Miss All Sunday
dialogue.text: Abducting the King was probably a piece of cake for them!

dialogue.speaker: Tashigi
dialogue.text: Listen up! The Alabasta you knew and loved is now dead!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: And the time to sweep away its rotting carcass has come!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: We will launch a full- scale attack on the Capital!

summary: This part develops the bananawani threat and pushes the Alabasta conflict closer to its next decisive turn.
